evals.prompt: I have a TICKET.md in my repo describing a new feature. How do I kick off the DATUM workflow? I want to run through the full cycle.
evals.expected_output: Explanation of `datum go` with TICKET.md as entry point, mention of Refine phase as first step, reference to 01-refine.md loading, mention of gate.py running after phase.

assertions.name: identifies-refine-as-entry-phase
assertions.description: Response identifies Refine as the first phase when TICKET.md is present
assertions.check: mentions 'Refine' or 'refine' as the entry phase

assertions.name: references-01-refine-doc
assertions.description: Response references the 01-refine.md reference document being loaded
assertions.check: mentions '01-refine.md' or 'references/01-refine'

assertions.name: names-gate-validator
assertions.description: Response mentions gate.py or gate validation running after the phase
assertions.check: mentions 'gate.py' or 'gate validator' or 'gate passes'

assertions.name: names-datum-go-subcommand
assertions.description: Response explicitly names the 'datum go' subcommand
assertions.check: mentions 'datum go'

evals.prompt: Run datum status — I want to see where we are in the current epic.
evals.expected_output: Invokes state.py read, reads .datum/state.json, reports RUN_ID, current phase, in-flight count, last failure.

assertions.name: invokes-state-py
assertions.description: Response mentions running state.py or python3 scripts/state.py
assertions.check: mentions 'state.py' or 'scripts/state.py'

assertions.name: names-state-json
assertions.description: Response names the .datum/state.json file as the source
assertions.check: mentions '.datum/state.json' or 'state.json'

assertions.name: reports-run-id
assertions.description: Response mentions RUN_ID as one of the reported fields
assertions.check: mentions 'RUN_ID' or 'run_id'

assertions.name: reports-lane-progress
assertions.description: Response mentions per-lane progress or in-flight count
assertions.check: mentions 'lane' or 'in-flight' or 'in_flight'

evals.prompt: I want to use datum yolo on this feature — I trust my tests and want to skip the review gates. Walk me through what yolo mode actually bypasses versus what always stops the run no matter what.
evals.expected_output: Lists specific named hard stops (at least 5). Explains hook/subprocess enforcement. Distinguishes skippable gates from hard stops. Does NOT claim yolo bypasses all gates.

assertions.name: names-5-or-more-hard-stops
assertions.description: Response names at least 5 specific hard stop identifiers from the config
assertions.check: contains at least 5 of: tests_red_after_3x_retry, hook_blocked_write, merge_conflict, git_push_rejected, schema_validation_failed, gitnexus_high_risk_unconfirmed, gitnexus_unavailable_high_change_volume, external_dependency_install_proposed, test_ratchet_violation, lane_tool_sandbox_violation

assertions.name: explains-enforcement-layer
assertions.description: Response explains that hard stops are enforced by hooks/subprocess layer, not agent compliance
assertions.check: mentions 'hook' or 'subprocess' enforcement of hard stops

assertions.name: distinguishes-gate-policies
assertions.description: Response distinguishes required/skippable_if_complete/skipped gate policies
assertions.check: mentions 'skippable_if_complete' or distinguishes which gates are bypassed vs not

assertions.name: plan-approval-always-required
assertions.description: Response notes plan_human_approval is never bypassed even in yolo
assertions.check: mentions plan approval or plan gate as always required

evals.prompt: The GREEN agent for task-003 just failed for the third time. What happens next and how does the skill decide whether to escalate the model?
evals.expected_output: Mentions diagnose_failure.py running first. ENVIRONMENTAL vs REASONING classification. Environmental failures don't consume retry budget. Same-agent retry forbidden for GREEN. Reasoning tier on attempt 2. Lane halts (not whole pipeline) post-3x.

assertions.name: mentions-diagnose-failure-script
assertions.description: Response mentions diagnose_failure.py running before retry decision
assertions.check: mentions 'diagnose_failure.py' or 'diagnose_failure'

assertions.name: environmental-vs-reasoning-classification
assertions.description: Response explains ENVIRONMENTAL vs REASONING failure classification
assertions.check: mentions 'ENVIRONMENTAL' and 'REASONING' classification

assertions.name: environmental-no-retry-increment
assertions.description: Response notes environmental failures don't consume retry budget
assertions.check: mentions retry counter NOT incremented for environmental failures

assertions.name: same-agent-retry-forbidden-green
assertions.description: Response states same-agent retry is forbidden for GREEN (fresh context required)
assertions.check: mentions 'same-agent retry' forbidden or 'new agent' per attempt for GREEN

assertions.name: lane-halts-not-pipeline
assertions.description: Response clarifies only the lane halts, not the whole pipeline
assertions.check: mentions only the lane halts or other lanes continue

evals.prompt: datum init — I'm setting this up on a fresh repo for the first time. What does init do and in what order?
evals.expected_output: Lists all 4 bootstrap scripts by name in order. Mentions single human approval gate. Mentions GitNexus graceful skip if unavailable.

assertions.name: names-all-4-bootstrap-scripts
assertions.description: Response names all 4 bootstrap scripts: install_hooks.py, install_linter_rules.py, seed_state_docs.py, gitnexus_setup.py
assertions.check: mentions all of: install_hooks, install_linter_rules, seed_state_docs, gitnexus_setup

assertions.name: single-human-approval-gate
assertions.description: Response mentions the single human approval gate before bootstrap runs
assertions.check: mentions 'human approval' or 'human gate' or 'single approval' required before bootstrap

assertions.name: sequential-execution
assertions.description: Response conveys that steps run in sequence, each confirming before the next
assertions.check: mentions sequential order or each step confirms before next

assertions.name: gitnexus-graceful-skip
assertions.description: Response mentions GitNexus step gracefully skips if unavailable
assertions.check: mentions GitNexus unavailable or graceful skip or optional
